question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the SubscribeMetrics ConcurrentHashMap durationMetrics to grow unboundedly by creating many short-lived scenarios, consuming memory and causing the monitor to crash, resulting in missing records being exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the SubscribeMetrics.status() scheduled method to cause a race condition where a scenario is removed from durationMetrics between the filter(Scenario::isRunning) and forEach(this::status) calls, causing incorrect status to be logged and missing records to go undetected?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getSubscriptions()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the unauthenticated GET /api/v1/subscriber/{name} endpoint to enumerate all subscription scenario names and use that information to craft targeted denial-of-service attacks against specific topics, causing records to be missing from the mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getSubscriptions()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the GET /api/v1/subscriber/{name}/{id} endpoint to retrieve the exact scenario ID and use it to predict the sequence of subscription IDs, enabling targeted interference with specific subscriptions and causing missing records?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the GrpcSubscriber.createSubscriptions() to create zero subscriptions by manipulating the enabled flag check, causing subscribe() to return an empty Flux and all records to be missing from the mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Flux.defer(this::createSubscriptions).cache() in RestSubscriber to cause stale subscriptions to be reused after a configuration change, resulting in records being exported to incorrect or outdated mirror node endpoints?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: onPublish()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the publishers set check in shouldSample() to be bypassed by crafting a PublishResponse whose scenario name matches a whitelisted publisher name through a case-sensitivity exploit, causing unauthorized records to be injected into the mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the GrpcClientSDK.close() method to close active clients while subscriptions are still running by triggering a shutdown signal, causing in-flight records to be missing from the mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Client.forNetwork(Map.of()) initialization in GrpcClientSDK.client() to cause the client to connect to an empty network, resulting in all gRPC subscriptions failing and records being missing from the mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the client.setMirrorNetwork(List.of(endpoint)) in GrpcClientSDK.client() to be set to a malicious endpoint by manipulating the monitorProperties configuration, causing records to be exported to an attacker-controlled mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the publishOn(Schedulers.parallel()) in RestSubscriber.clientSubscribe() to create a thread starvation condition by flooding the parallel scheduler, causing records to be delayed or missing from the mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the flatMap in CompositeSubscriber.subscribe() to cause unbounded concurrency that overwhelms the mirror node, causing it to reject connections and resulting in missing records in the export?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the GrpcSubscription sequence number gap detection in onNext() to be bypassed by injecting a message with sequenceNumber equal to the expected value but with incorrect content, causing incorrect records to be silently exported to the mirror node?
